# §Goal

## Canon

A first-class target-state type (M19): distinct from a static Requirement because it carries a MOVING TARGET that yields a Gap driving a Process.

## Narrative

A Goal is a target-state predicate (kind in TARGET_KINDS: GRAPH_PROPERTY | BUSINESS_STATE | ENTITY_STATE) plus what it targets. It is distinct from a static Requirement claim because it carries a MOVING TARGET — the distance between the target_state and the current state is a Gap, and that Gap drives Process execution as the measurable work remaining.

## Why

WHY a type (M19), not a Requirement facet: a Requirement is a static claim ('the system shall ...'), but a target that moves ('reach state X by ...') needs a current-vs-target Gap to drive a Process. Promoting Goal to its own type keeps the static-claim semantics of Requirement clean. Goal conflicts reuse the existing Conflict connector node on a goal-tension axis — no new GoalConflict type.
